desc: Mostar is one of the Balkans' most striking historic cities, centered on the iconic Stari Most arching above the Neretva and an atmospheric Ottoman-era bazaar quarter. It is compact, highly photogenic, and easy to pair with Blagaj, Počitelj, and Kravice on short regional loops, with strongest comfort in spring and early autumn before midsummer heat and day-trip crowding peak.

todo.name: Stari Most (Old Bridge)
todo.desc: The 16th-century Ottoman bridge is one of Europe's most beautiful sights — best at dusk when the stone turns amber.

todo.name: Kujundžiluk bazaar
todo.desc: Ancient cobblestoned market street lined with copper smiths, hand-woven rugs and coffee houses. Very authentic.

todo.name: Koski Mehmed Pasha Mosque
todo.desc: Climb the minaret for the finest panorama over the Old Bridge and Neretva valley.

todo.name: Blagaj Tekke
todo.desc: A 16th-century Dervish monastery at the foot of a 200 m cliff where the Buna river springs from rock. Unmissable.

todo.name: Počitelj village
todo.desc: Perfectly preserved medieval Ottoman fortified village perched above the Neretva. 25 min south.

todo.name: Kravice waterfalls
todo.desc: A 25 m natural amphitheatre of cascades into a turquoise pool — Herzegovina's most beautiful natural site.

todo.name: Neretva river swimming
todo.desc: The crystal-clear Neretva below the Old Bridge is swimmable in summer and startlingly beautiful.

todo.name: Herzegovinian wine tasting
todo.desc: Žilavka white and Blatina red are indigenous grapes found nowhere else. Winery visits around Čitluk are excellent.

wifi.note: Usually adequate for remote work in central hotels and cafés, though reliability is less consistent than in larger regional capitals.

prac.fltNote: No routine nonstop Gatwick service to Mostar (OMO). Most UK itineraries route via Split (SPU), Dubrovnik (DBV), or Sarajevo (SJJ) with onward road transfer. Return fares are often ~£60–£150 off-peak, ~£150–£260 in shoulder demand, and ~£260+ around summer and holiday peaks.
prac.visa: UK and US passport holders can generally enter visa-free for short stays (commonly up to 90 days in 180 days).
alerts: Midday heat can be intense in July-August; plan bridge-area walking for early morning or evening.
Popular viewpoints and bridge approaches become crowded when cruise and coastal day-trip buses arrive.
Cross-border day trips toward Croatia can be slower in peak summer and holiday windows.

sweet: May-June and September are usually best for warm weather, lower crowd pressure, and easier day-trip logistics than peak midsummer.
